What we do

One platform for every inch
of your operation

Ranch Systems brings irrigation automation, environmental monitoring, and remote equipment control under a single cloud platform — so you spend less time driving and more time growing.

Irrigation & Pump Control

Open and close valves, start and stop pumps, and schedule irrigation cycles — all from the myRanch app without setting foot in the field.

Learn more →

Soil Moisture Monitoring

Track real-time soil moisture at multiple depths. Get instant alerts when conditions fall outside target ranges so you never over- or under-water.

Learn more →

Weather & Climate Monitoring

On-site weather stations feed real-time data directly into your irrigation decisions — temperature, humidity, wind speed, and ET calculations.

Learn more →

Remote Cameras

Keep eyes on remote equipment, harvest progress, and field conditions. Live video feeds and time-lapse from anywhere with cellular coverage.

Learn more →

Tank & Pond Monitoring

Monitor water levels in tanks, ponds, and reservoirs continuously. Automate fill cycles and receive low-level alerts before you run dry.

Learn more →

Alerts & Notifications

Instant SMS, email, and push alerts for equipment faults, threshold breaches, and system events — before small problems become costly ones.

Learn more →

How it works

Live in three steps

From box to dashboard, most growers are up and running the same week.

1

Install the hardware

Mount a solar-powered telemetry unit at your site. No electrician needed for most installs, no WiFi required.

2

Connect to myRanch

Your unit connects over cellular straight to the myRanch cloud dashboard — usually live within minutes.

3

Automate & monitor

Build irrigation programs, set alert thresholds, and check every site from your phone. myRanch handles the rest.

The myRanch platform

Your entire operation,
one dashboard

The myRanch cloud platform aggregates sensor data, control events, weather readings, and camera feeds into a single interface — accessible on desktop, tablet, or phone.

Remote valve & pump controlManual overrides and scheduled automation from any device
Configurable alert thresholdsCustom notifications by sensor, zone, or equipment type
Historical data & reportingTrend analysis and downloadable reports for water compliance
Cellular & satellite connectivityReliable data from even the most remote field locations

Built for growers everywhere

From a single block to
thousands of acres

Founded in Novato, California in 2005, Ranch Systems now supports operations across North America, Europe, and beyond — adapting to local crops, climates, and water regulations wherever you farm.

Global Deployments

Operations across the US, Spain, and international markets — one platform, many climates.

Any Scale

From small specialty farms to large multi-site enterprise growers and water agencies.

Remote-First

Cellular and satellite connectivity keeps even off-grid sites online and controllable.

Always-On Support

A dedicated reseller network and support team backing your hardware in the field.

Proven across crops

Before you reach out

A few common questions

Yes. RS130, RS330, and RM400 all connect over cellular, so most sites never need WiFi at all.

Usually not for the telemetry hardware itself — units are solar and battery powered. Valve or pump control wiring may need a licensed electrician depending on your setup.

Each unit keeps logging locally, so readings aren't lost — once signal returns, the backlog syncs automatically and myRanch flags any device that went offline.

Yes — RM400 and RS330 both support expansion boards, and you can add new units to the same myRanch account as your operation grows.

See all FAQs →

Home  /  Solutions  /  Agriculture

Agriculture Solutions

Every sensor and control
your farm needs, on one platform

From weather and soil moisture to irrigation, pumps, tanks, cameras, and alerts — RanchMaster brings the full cycle of agricultural monitoring and control into a single cloud application you can run from anywhere.

Request a Demo See the Modules

Weather & Climate Monitoring

A complete weather station solution with accurate, continuous, real-time recording of temperature, humidity, wind, rain, solar radiation, and leaf wetness. Built-in analytics include disease indices, degree days, chill portions, and wet bulb — typically paired with alerts to warn growers of dangerous conditions the moment they develop.

A single wireless base station can act as a full-function weather station while coordinating other nodes across the property, so you can add microclimate monitoring, frost alerting, or irrigation control as needs grow.

Features

  • Temperature, humidity, wind, rain, solar, UV, leaf wetness
  • Continuous real-time transmission to online software
  • Disease indices: powdery mildew & botrytis
  • Degree days, chill hours, chill portions, wet bulb

Benefits

  • Reduce water use by irrigating to climate
  • Detect frost and excessive-heat risk early
  • Reduce spraying with local disease indices
  • Improve long-term crop selection by microclimate
Vineyard Station 2 · live
61°F
Air temp
72%
Humidity
6 mph
Wind NW
0.3"
Rain today
Powdery mildew index — 14 days

Soil Moisture Monitoring

Measure soil moisture continuously and in real time at multiple depths and multiple points across a property. Use the data to schedule irrigation, trigger critical-level alerts, or build a smarter irrigation basis by combining it with climate and water-resource information.

Because moisture varies widely with soil type and terrain, multiple measurement points keep your picture accurate. View readings as a time graph or as a unique color-coded "Soil Moisture Profiler" showing a cross-section of the profile over time.

Features

  • Single or multi-level probes at many points
  • Support for a wide range of third-party probes
  • Continuous real-time transmission to RanchMaster
  • Time graph or color-coded soil-water profile

Benefits

  • Reduce water use by knowing availability
  • Improve quality by managing plant stress
  • Develop larger root systems safely
  • Cut labor and service cost of manual readings
myRanch · Fruit Orchard soil zone

Irrigation Valve Control

Irrigating large, multiple, or remote properties is labor-intensive, and it is often hard to know whether valves actually opened or the right amount of water was delivered. RanchMaster manages the full cycle — valve activation, flow measurement, and soil-moisture tracking — for monitoring only or full remote control and scheduling.

Features

  • Schedule irrigation online from anywhere
  • Base station automatically controls valves
  • Wireless nodes reach remote manifolds
  • Flow sensors provide feedback & fault alerts

Benefits

  • Lower labor costs
  • Greater accuracy with smaller, frequent sets
  • Reduce water use with night irrigation
Irrigation zones · 4 active
Zone 1 — North blockOn · 22 min left
Zone 2 — HillsideOn · 41 min left
Zone 3 — Lower rowScheduled 4:00 AM
Zone 4 — East blockIdle
Flow rate214 gpm

Pump & Engine Control

Remotely monitor and control pumps — electric or engine-driven — from simple on/off pressure monitoring to full engine statistics with start/stop. The most advanced control comes through the Ranch Systems partnership with Murphy, letting any suitable Murphy-controlled engine be run online.

Features

  • Control electric to large engine pumps
  • Closed-loop start monitoring with failure alerts
  • Remote diagnostics and re-configuration
  • Auto-start on frost, low-water, or moisture

Benefits

  • Save labor and travel on manual operation
  • Cut energy by running off-peak
  • Prevent wear and dry-running damage
  • Optimize refueling from exact run-times

Explore Remote Engine Control →

Well pump 3 · running
RPM1,780
Discharge pressure42 psi
Engine temp186°F
Run hours1,204 h
StatusNominal

Tank & Pond Monitoring

Monitor the level of tanks, ponds, wells, and other liquid storage — including fuel and fertilizer — using submersible pressure or ultrasonic sensors. The software calculates volume from tank geometry, and alerts can even trigger a shutoff valve or alarm horn automatically.

Features

  • Submersible pressure & ultrasonic level sensors
  • Real-time level and current gallons per tank
  • One-click graphs of level or volume over time
  • Alerts on out-of-range conditions; PDF reports

Benefits

  • Reduce risk and cost of outages
  • Improve delivery-truck scheduling
  • Prevent overfills and overflows
  • Track water usage
Reservoir 1 · 38% — low alert armed
Storage levels
Reservoir 138%  ·  41,800 gal
Tank A82%  ·  9,840 gal
Tank B66%  ·  7,920 gal
Fertilizer54%  ·  1,620 gal

Remote Cameras

Unlike web-cams that stream constantly, RanchMaster cameras capture still images at intervals — or on a motion or keypad event — and economically transmit them to the cloud. Images appear in a calendar view, making it easy to pinpoint activity at a site, track crop development, or feed a live snapshot to your own website.

Features

  • Still images on motion, keypad, or interval
  • Calendar view of images & access events
  • Image-content timeseries (e.g. fog patterns)
  • Email/smartphone alert with captured image

Benefits

  • Record visual crop development over time
  • Track fieldwork remotely
  • Deter theft and increase security
  • Embed a live image into your own website
RanchCam · 6 sites
Latest captures
North gate7:15 AM · interval
Pump houseMotion 6:48 AM
Block C row7:00 AM · interval
Reservoir7:15 AM · interval

Alerts & Notifications

Early detection of problem conditions, with rules and recipients defined by you. Triggers can be as simple as "temperature < 38" for frost or a complex calculated formula. Delivery is by email, text, or voice, and the system can call multiple recipients repeatedly until one acknowledges — minutes can mean millions.

Features

  • User-defined simple or formula-based alerts
  • Delivery by email, SMS, or voice call
  • User-definable alert text, multiple recipients
  • Online tracking of recipient confirmations

Benefits

  • Reduce frost crop losses with fast response
  • Catch irrigation failures early
  • Prevent tank run-outs and overfills

Home  /  Solutions  /  Water Management

Water Management Solutions

Continuous, remote
monitoring for water agencies

Two decades of helping farms manage water, now applied to agencies and infrastructure. Monitor surface water, wells, pipelines, and groundwater — and control pumps — through one open platform that talks to SCADA, Modbus, PLCs, and more.

Request a Demo See Capabilities

Surface Water

Surface water is shaped by weather, pollution, agriculture, and land use, which increasingly demands continuous monitoring. Ranch Systems telemetry performs hydrological and water-quality monitoring of fresh water — still water like lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, as well as the running water of streams and rivers.

We integrate a wide range of sensors: multi-parameter sondes, submersible and radar level, and bubblers, plus a full range of adjunct weather stations. Systems can support flood-warning and stormwater applications.

Features

  • Hydrological measurement of level and flow
  • Water-quality monitoring with multi-parameter sondes
  • Ancillary weather monitoring

Benefits

  • Frequent access to detailed data
  • Long-term cost savings
  • No manual collection or remote travel
River station 4 · live
Stage & discharge — 24 h
Stage3.4 ft
Discharge118 cfs

Water Infrastructure

The same experience that helped large farms manage irrigation now helps agencies manage infrastructure. Remotely and continuously monitor tanks, ponds, reservoirs, and wells; track pipeline pressure and flow; and monitor and control pump stations. Modbus-compatible and SCADA-friendly, with open architecture that fits your existing systems.

Features

  • Tank, pond, and reservoir levels
  • Pipeline pressure and flow
  • Well monitoring, including quality
  • Pump-station monitoring & control; SCADA integration

Benefits

  • Address issues before they become problems
  • Improve monitoring and management
  • Reduce costs through labor savings
  • Better meet reporting requirements
District network · 12 stations
Pump station 2Running
Pipeline pressure64 psi
Reservoir East71% full
Well 7 level128 ft
SCADA linkConnected

Groundwater

Significant experience monitoring both water-supply wells and groundwater monitoring wells. Track level with submersible or ultrasonic sensors and discharge through any pulse-output flow meter. Our pump-control expertise extends to electric motors, VFDs, and even diesel engines with Modbus controllers, and a wide range of water-quality sensors can be applied.

Features

  • In-situ, real-time level and/or quality
  • Compatible with a wide range of sensors
  • Pump control and discharge measurement

Benefits

  • Better groundwater management long term
  • Helps determine influences and use
  • Estimates recharge rates
Monitoring well GW-3
Groundwater level — 30 days
Depth to water142 ft
Discharge340 gpm

Alerting, Reporting & Dashboards

Advanced decision support built into the online application. Alerting is quick to set up yet can be triggered by any sensor — or "virtual" sensor data based on multiple inputs and algorithms. Custom reports generate and deliver automatically, data exports easily, and customizable dashboards with widgets surface exactly what an operation needs to act on.

Features

  • Easy text, voice, and email alerts
  • Customizable reports for export or auto-email
  • Customizable decision-support dashboards

Benefits

  • Early detection and failure prevention
  • Automated administration of monitoring data
  • More effective, timely decisions
Custom dashboard · 12 stations
Auto report — daily extractionScheduled
Alert — high flow line 4Armed
ExportCSV · PDF
Recipients4 staff

Also available for agencies

Soil moisture and weather, on the same platform

Soil Moisture

Hundreds of installs of experience, compatible with the sensors found across the market and installed to give accurate, meaningful data. View it with best-in-class stacked graphs featuring color banding and annotation, plus year-over-year comparison. Stations expand easily with weather sensors.

Weather & Climate

Open architecture lets agencies configure stations to their requirements and budget — from a basic station with telemetry to a meteorological-grade build. Augment any existing station with weather sensors, and import or export data from sources like CIMIS or Weather Underground.

Bring continuous monitoring to your water system

Home  /  Solutions  /  Remote Engine Control

Remote Engine Control & Monitoring

Control your engines
from anywhere

Pumps, wind machines, power units, and generators are just a few of the applications running on controllers. Murphy by Enovation Controls provides the right mix of features for your application — and combined with Ranch Systems telemetry, controlling and monitoring engines has never been easier.

Get Started Today Why Ranch Systems

Why Ranch Systems for my remote solution

One system for control, alerts, and reporting

Control from anywhere

Monitor and control from anywhere with cellular coverage through a secure, cloud-based portal — no trip to the field required.

Custom reports

Receive custom reports on usage and performance, so you always have a record of how each engine is running.

Customized alerts

Get alerts via SMS, email, or voice call on conditions such as alarms, high flow, or low pressure — the moment they happen.

One expandable system

Easily expand the integrated monitoring system to other field instruments — a single solution for all your monitoring and control needs.

Is my engine compatible?

Most engines can be retrofitted with a modern controller, such as the PowerCore® TEC-10 from Murphy by Enovation Controls. Paired with a Ranch Systems telemetry node, that turns an ordinary engine into one you can watch and run from your phone.

Add-on flow-meter monitoring is available for any flow meter with a pulse or 4–20mA output, so water delivery is tracked alongside engine performance.

Compatible telemetry

  • RS130-H standalone cellular telemetry node
  • RS300-H expandable cellular node
  • RS300-V expandable cellular node

Remote control modes

  • RPM
  • Pressure maintain · pressure start/stop
  • Level maintain · level start/stop
Diesel pump · TEC-10 + RS130
RPM1,650
Oil pressure48 psi
Engine temp192°F
Discharge pressure55 psi
Run hours3,410 h
Remote controlStart / Stop ready

How the control works

From a click online to the engine, in under a minute

A Murphy controller connects to a Ranch Systems node over RS-485. The node pulls engine data at regular intervals and delivers it to the online application over cellular. When you start or stop — manually, on a schedule, or by an automated rule reacting to another sensor — the command travels back to the node and to the controller, typically in under a minute.

Live telemetry

Engine status current within 5–10 minutes: RPM, temperature, oil pressure, battery, run hours, discharge pressure, and intake level indicators.

Fast control

Start/stop delay under 5 seconds when pre-programmed, or under a minute ad-hoc, with remote throttling where the controller supports it.

Rule-based automation

Program auto start/stop based on other sensors and critical conditions — frost, low water, soil moisture — as part of a full irrigation automation solution.

Let's find the right controller for your engine

Home  /  Solutions  /  Water Meter Monitoring

Automated Water Meter Monitoring & Reporting

Meet extraction reporting
rules automatically

Capture flow data from wells and present it in LiveMap, spreadsheet, or PDF formats. Reports can be customized from a library of templates, then generated and emailed automatically — so regulatory compliance happens in the background.

Request a Demo How It Works

Reporting for regulatory requirements

The Ranch Systems software reporting feature easily captures flow data from wells and displays it in the LiveMap, spreadsheet, or PDF formats. Reports can be customized from a library of available templates, generated and emailed automatically — fulfilling extraction and regulatory reporting requirements without manual effort.

Related project: Fox Canyon Groundwater Management Agency AMI →

Extraction report · auto-generated
Well 1 — monthly total412 AF
Well 2 — monthly total288 AF
Well 3 — monthly total196 AF
FormatLiveMap · CSV · PDF
DeliveryEmailed monthly

Labor savings through remote pump control

Labor costs are rising and skilled workers are scarcer. Remotely turning pumps on and off — especially in the evening or on a Sunday — saves cost and adds convenience. Where it makes sense, Ranch Systems can determine whether a pump can be automated to run on schedule or on demand, reducing overall farming costs.

Pump scheduling
Well pump 1Off-peak 10 PM–6 AM
Well pump 2Running
Sunday scheduleAutomated
Manual overrideAvailable

Continuous monitoring and alerting

The cloud-based solution collects real-time data and makes it available through an online application on smartphone, tablet, or computer — letting water-market participants automate monitoring of field flow meters. A powerful alerting function notifies users of any critical condition via text, email, or phone, and can be configured for additional alerts as needed.

Field meters · 8 reporting
Flow — last 24 h
Current flow186 gpm
Alert — high flowArmed

Accurate support for existing meters

Most installed water meters do not output an accurate meter value, and those that do often produce only pulses — which create serious compliance problems. Replacing meters is a major expense. The Ranch Systems Optical Reader solves this by electronically reading the meter face of virtually any existing meter and making the value available online.

Optical Reader · Meter 4
Meter face reading0084216 gal
Reader typeOptical (no pulse)
Existing meterKept in place
Online valueLive

Automate your water meter reporting

Home  /  Solutions  /  Heat Alert Solution

Heat Alert Solution

Keep your crews safe
when the heat climbs

A complete software and hardware package for farm and labor managers to monitor conditions while workers are in the field — real-time temperature, humidity, and calculated heat index, with automatic alerts and the reporting needed to satisfy regulators.

Request a Demo How It Works

Monitor real-time field conditions

The cloud-based Heat Alert Solution collects real-time data, accessed through myRanch on smartphone, tablet, or computer. Managers see site-specific conditions that may affect worker health, and a powerful, customizable alerting function notifies them of critical conditions via text, email, or phone. Weekly weather reports arrive automatically by email for later reference and auditing.

What it monitors

  • Real-time temperature
  • Relative humidity
  • Calculated heat index
  • Telemetry station health

How it alerts

  • SMS / text messages
  • App notifications
  • Email and voice call
  • Station-issue alerts
Field 3 · heat index rising
96°F
Air temp
48%
Humidity
104°F
Heat index
High
Risk level
SMS sent to crew leads3 recipients
AcknowledgedM. Lopez · 1:24 PM

Compliance made simple

Documentation and reporting, handled

Long-term documentation of field-specific temperatures and conditions makes it easy to generate reports — like a monthly log of interval temperatures during the day for any site — and distribute them automatically by email to satisfy reporting requirements. Managers can step away or be off-site and still keep an eye on the temperature.

Prevent worker health issues

Heat alerts prevent or minimize worker health problems caused by extreme field conditions, giving managers time to act before risk becomes harm.

Satisfy regulations

The system supports compliance with government heat-illness requirements through automatic, long-term documentation and reporting.

Automatic weekly reports

Weekly weather reports are delivered automatically by email, ready for reference or auditing whenever they're needed.

Protect your crews this season

Home  /  Products  /  Telemetry

Telemetry

Hardware built for the field

Five units cover every property size — from a single solar-powered RS130 watching one tank, to a mesh network of RS330 units spanning a multi-block ranch. All of them speak the same language: Ranch Cloud™ and the myRanch app.

Request a Demo See the Hardware

Telemetry units

Three nodes, one platform

Each unit reads sensors, logs data, and connects to myRanch — the difference is how much they control and how far they reach.

Monitoring & Control Unit

RM400

The flagship controller — telemetry, data logging, and valve control for up to 48 valves in one unit.

View RM400 →
Cellular Telemetry Unit

RS130

A self-contained, solar-powered field node for a single site — soil moisture, weather, tanks, or pumps.

View RS130 →
Cellular or Mesh Telemetry Unit

RS330

Standalone over cellular, or linked into a RanchMesh™ network for large or cellular-poor properties.

View RS330 →

*Number of supported valves and exact port counts vary by configuration. Contact sales for a configuration matched to your site.

Expansion units

Go wireless with RS10

Not every sensor needs a cable run — connect wirelessly, then add a receiver to any telemetry unit without built-in Bluetooth®.

Sensor Transmitter & Data Logger

RS10

A compact, solar-powered Bluetooth® transmitter that connects to SDI-12 sensors and relays data up to 1,000 ft.

View RS10 →
Sensor Data Receiver

RS10 Receiver

Adds Bluetooth® connectivity to any telemetry unit that doesn't have it built in, powered by the host unit.

View RS10 Receiver →

Why the hardware holds up

Built for the field, not the showroom

Outdoor-rated & solar-powered

Impact-resistant, UV-stabilized enclosures with onboard solar charging keep units running unattended through heat, dust, and weather.

Cellular, WiFi, or mesh — your choice

Connect a single node over cellular, or link multiple units into a RanchMesh™ network when coverage is limited or a site is large.

Modular expansion as you grow

Add relay boards, sensor inputs, or serial ports later — the same patented platform scales from one tank to a farm-wide network.

Home  /  Products  /  myRanch

myRanch

Your operation, one login

Every sensor, valve, pump, and camera you connect reports into the myRanch cloud dashboard — accessible from a phone, tablet, or browser. Build automated irrigation programs in minutes, override anything in real time, and let alerts tell you the moment something needs attention.

Request a Demo See the Hardware

One dashboard, every site

myRanch brings every connected device — across every site — into a single login. See live status, jump into any zone, and manage your whole operation from wherever you are.

On the dashboard

  • Live status across every connected site
  • Automated, optimized irrigation programs
  • Historical trends & compliance reports
  • Manual override from anywhere

Available on

  • Desktop & tablet browser
  • myRanch mobile app
  • SMS, email & voice alerts
  • Cellular & WiFi connected devices
myRanch · 3 sites connected
North Block — VineyardOnline
Well 4 — Pump StationOnline
East Tank — ReservoirLow Level
14
Active devices
99.8%
Uptime, 30 days

Set it and trust it

Automation that adjusts itself

Build a program once — myRanch keeps it optimized as conditions change.

Shortest possible run time

Programs are automatically optimized so equipment runs only as long as it needs to.

Adjusts to real conditions

Programs dynamically adapt to real-time environmental data instead of running on a fixed schedule.

Alerts that reach you

SMS, email, or voice alerts the moment a device goes offline, a tank runs low, or a program needs attention.

One app, two modes

Real-time telemetry or data logger — same myRanch

Every device reports into myRanch, whether it's transmitting live or logging for later download.

Real-time telemetry

  • RM400, RS130, and RS330 — live in myRanch the moment a device connects
  • Built for active monitoring and remote control

RanchDL (store & download)

  • RS10 running in data-logger mode instead of real-time transmission
  • A lower-cost option for sites that don't need continuous live data
  • Logs locally for download on your own schedule, still through myRanch
  • Download logged data at RanchDL →

Home  /  Products  /  RM400

Telemetry Units

RM400 Monitoring & Control Unit

The flagship controller in the Ranch Systems lineup — a telemetry node, data logger, and valve controller in one, built to be the backbone of your monitoring and control system.

Request a Demo See Features
Monitoring & Control Unit

Overview

The RM400 is an advanced telemetry node, data logger, and valve controller capable of reading data from a variety of sensors and uploading it to the Ranch Cloud™ platform. It's built to be the backbone of your monitoring and control system, with a built-in display and keypad for in-field configuration, valve control, and data readout — plus advanced configuration over a cabled PC connection or the myRanch™ app.

RM400 can control up to 48 valves* and accepts multiple sensor input types. In the myRanch app, build fully automated irrigation programs in minutes — automatically optimized for the shortest run time, and dynamically adjusted for real-time conditions. It supports both WiFi and cellular connectivity, and samples traditional wired sensors as well as sensors connected via the RS10 Bluetooth® Transmitter, making new and upgraded installations easier.

*Number of supported valves varies by configuration.

Built in

RM400 features

Hardware & connectivity

  • Outdoor-rated, impact-resistant, UV-stabilized polycarbonate enclosure
  • OLED display w/ keypad interface
  • 10 to 24V AC/DC flex power input
  • 3.7V Li-Ion rechargeable battery
  • USB-C cable interface for PC connection
  • Bluetooth® modem
  • WiFi modem

Monitoring & control

  • ARM 32-bit CPU w/ 4MB flash file system for data buffering
  • Real-time OS supporting Ranch Systems JavaScript for custom integrations
  • Four status LEDs
  • 5V and 12V DC excitation voltage generator
  • Eight (8) feedback sensor/digital counter ports
  • Onboard diagnostics: internal temperature, battery voltage, current draw, charge current, cellular/WiFi signal strength

Grow as you go

Optional expansion components

  • Cellular modem w/ AGPS
  • 24VAC solenoid control output, up to 48 outputs w/ four (4) stackable, 12-port expansion boards
  • Sensor input expansion board

Ready to put RM400 to work?

Home  /  Products  /  RS130

Telemetry Units

RS130 Cellular Telemetry Unit

A self-contained, solar-powered telemetry node and data logger — built for ease of use, with a built-in OLED display and included remote control for in-field configuration and read-out.

Request a Demo See Features
Cellular Telemetry Unit

Overview

The RS130 is an advanced telemetry node and data logger capable of reading data from a variety of sensors and uploading it to the Ranch Cloud™ platform. The unit is focused on ease of use, with a built-in OLED display and infrared remote control for in-field configuration and data read-out. Advanced configuration is available over a cabled PC connection or the myRanch™ app.

RS130 doesn't have built-in Bluetooth®, so it pairs with the RS10 Bluetooth® Receiver when you want to add wireless sensors via the RS10 Transmitter.

Built in

RS130 features

Hardware

  • Powder-coated machined aluminum base with heavy-duty, impact-resistant SAN plastic dome cover
  • OLED display
  • Infrared remote control (included)
  • Two 3.7V 2.6Ah Li-Ion rechargeable batteries
  • 360° flexible solar panel w/ advanced MPPT solar charge controller

Connectivity & monitoring

  • Cellular modem w/ AGPS
  • ARM 32-bit CPU w/ 4MB flash file system for data buffering
  • Real-time OS supporting Ranch Systems JavaScript for custom integrations
  • Onboard diagnostics: internal temperature, battery voltage, current draw, charge current, cellular signal strength
  • 5V and 12V DC excitation voltage generator
  • Sensor input expansion board (varies by configuration)

Ready to put RS130 to work?

Home  /  Products  /  RS330

Telemetry Units

RS330 Cellular or Mesh Telemetry Unit

An advanced telemetry node, data logger, and equipment controller that runs standalone over cellular, or as part of a multi-node RanchMesh™ network for large or cellular-poor properties.

Request a Demo See Features
Cellular or Mesh Telemetry Unit

Overview

The RS330 is an advanced telemetry node, data logger, and equipment controller capable of reading data from a variety of sensors and uploading it to the Ranch Cloud™ platform. Configuration is available over a cabled PC connection or the myRanch™ app.

RS330 can function as a standalone unit or as part of a multi-node RanchMesh™ network. The standalone unit includes a cellular modem that communicates directly with Ranch Cloud, so you can place it wherever cellular service is available. A RanchMesh network is the better fit for areas with limited cellular coverage, or for large installations spanning multiple locations.

Standalone or networked

How RanchMesh™ works

Base station

  • Includes a cellular modem to communicate with Ranch Cloud
  • Includes a local-area mesh modem to communicate with other nodes
  • Every RanchMesh installation requires at least one base station

Router & end nodes

  • Communicate with the base station and each other over the local mesh
  • End nodes can reach the base station directly, or relay through router nodes
  • This multi-hop approach extends range past line-of-sight or terrain limits

Built in

RS330 features

Hardware & power

  • Heavy-duty, outdoor-rated all-metal enclosure
  • Integrated high-efficiency 5W or 10W monocrystalline solar panel w/ aluminum frame
  • 12V 2Ah or 4Ah NiMH rechargeable battery
  • Modem & GPS (varies by configuration)
  • Cellular/GPS combo antenna, or outdoor-grade whip antenna for RanchMesh
  • Three status LEDs

Ports & monitoring

  • One analog 0-5V DC, digital counter, or frequency input port
  • One RS-232 port and one RS-485/SDI-12 port
  • Two port expansion slots, each with two physical ports
  • 5V and 12V DC excitation voltage generator
  • ARM 32-bit CPU w/ 4MB flash file system for data buffering
  • Onboard diagnostics: internal temperature, battery voltage, current draw, charge current, cellular/RanchMesh signal strength

Grow as you go

Optional expansion components

  • DC latching 12VDC relay expansion board (up to 4 control outputs via 2 physical ports)
  • Sensor input expansion board (up to 4 inputs via 2 physical ports — analog 0-5VDC, 5V or 12V constant power)
  • Serial communications expansion board (1 RS-232 port and 1 RS-485/SDI-12 port)
  • RS10 Bluetooth® Receiver for use with the RS10 Transmitter

Home  /  Products  /  RS10

Expansion Units

RS10 Sensor Transmitter & Data Logger

A compact, self-contained sensor interface using long-range, ultra-low-power Bluetooth® — connect sensors wirelessly to a host telemetry unit up to 1,000 feet away.

Request a Demo Sensor Compatibility
Sensor Transmitter & Data Logger

Overview

The RS10 is a compact, self-contained sensor interface utilizing Bluetooth® long-range, ultra-low-power wireless technology. It communicates with a host telemetry unit to upload sensor data and receive commands from the Ranch Cloud™ platform.

With RS10, equipment installation is no longer constrained by cabling — place sensors where needed and connect them wirelessly to a host unit up to 1,000 feet away (typically up to 350 ft. with trees or foliage in the way). It's well suited to trees/vines, row crops, pivot/linear irrigation, potted nursery, hoop house, and greenhouse environments, plus small-plot research and other environmental monitoring. Its small size and flexible mounting make it easy to move during harvest or whenever a cable run would be in the way.

RS10 connects directly to RM400 (built-in Bluetooth®), or to RS130 and RS330 via the RS10 Receiver.

Built in

RS10 features

  • Weatherproof polycarbonate enclosure
  • Bluetooth® modem
  • 3.6V 1000mAh NiMH rechargeable battery
  • Two (2) fixed solar panels
  • Status LED
  • 12V DC excitation voltage generator
  • One (1) SDI-12 sensor input port with splitter-cable support for multiple sensors
  • Onboard diagnostics: internal temperature, battery voltage, signal strength

Works with

Sensor compatibility

RS10 samples sensors using the SDI-12 protocol, with simultaneous connection of multiple sensor types.

Soil & moisture

  • Aquacheck Subsurface Soil Moisture
  • EnviroPro Soil Probe (with or without EC)
  • John Deere Field Connect Soil Moisture
  • Sentek Drill & Drop Soil Moisture & Temperature
  • Sentek Drill & Drop Bluetooth® Soil Moisture & Temperature
  • Sentek Drill & Drop w/ TriSCAN: Soil Moisture, Temperature & EC

Plant & environmental

  • FloraPulse Microtensiometer
  • HSTI HydraSCOUT Soil Moisture
  • METER Group ATMOS 14 Temperature/Humidity/Barometric Pressure
  • METER Group Teros 12 Soil Moisture, Temperature & EC
  • SDI-12 0-250 PSI Pressure Transducer
  • SDI-12 Air, Soil & Water Temperature, Leaf Wetness, Water Level

Don't see your sensor or manufacturer? Contact our sales team — we're always adding support for new sensor types.

Ready to go wireless?

Home  /  Products  /  RS10 Receiver

Expansion Units

RS10 Receiver

A compact, self-contained external modem that adds Bluetooth® connectivity to telemetry units that don't have it built in.

Request a Demo View RS10 Transmitter
Sensor Data Receiver

Overview

The RS10 Bluetooth® Receiver is a compact, self-contained external modem for telemetry units that don't have built-in Bluetooth®. Powered by the host telemetry unit, it lets that unit sample sensors connected via the RS10 Bluetooth® Transmitter & Data Logger — giving growers more options for upgrading or expanding an installation with wireless sensor connectivity.

RM400 has Bluetooth® built in and doesn't need a receiver. RS130 and RS330 both support the RS10 Receiver to add that connectivity.

Works with

Compatible telemetry units

RS130

Add the RS10 Receiver to connect RS10 wireless sensors.

View RS130 →

RS330

Available as an optional expansion component.

View RS330 →

RS10 Transmitter

The wireless sensor interface this receiver pairs with.

View RS10 →

Home  /  About Us  /  Who We Are

Who We Are

Farming roots.
Silicon Valley engineering.

Ranch Systems was founded in 2005 by Jacob Christfort, who paired a career in Silicon Valley wireless technology with generations of farming in his family to build a new kind of monitoring and control company. Two decades later, the same patented, modular platform that started with a single sensor now supports growers and water managers across North America, Latin America, Europe, and Australia.

Find a Reseller Our Story

Two decades of modular telemetry

Ranch Systems built its reputation in California agriculture, then began expanding internationally in 2008. What makes the platform different is its range: the same patented technology covers a single cellular node watching one tank at a competitive price, all the way to sophisticated farm-wide networks with real-time monitoring and control of an entire growing and irrigation cycle — cameras included. While agriculture remains the core focus, the same rugged, off-grid telemetry now serves environmental monitoring customers as well.

Where we started

  • Founded 2005 in Novato, CA
  • Family farming roots in Mendocino County
  • Leadership position in California agriculture

Where we are now

  • Growers across 4 world regions
  • Ranch Systems Iberica S.L.U. in Spain
  • Patented, modular hardware platform
Company timeline
2005Founded in Novato, California
2008Begins expanding internationally
2017Ranch Systems Iberica opens in Spain
TodayResellers across 4 world regions

Led by a rancher who builds technology

Before founding Ranch Systems, Jacob spent nearly a decade at Oracle, where he built the company's first mainstream wireless application server and went on to serve as its first CTO for mobile, wireless, and voice technology — also serving a term as Vice Chairman of the Open Mobile Allience's predecessor industry body. Earlier, he advised clients at McKinsey & Company, co-founded a Danish startup building rugged field computers for agriculture and forestry, and consulted on technology for the Danish government's foreign-aid program in West Africa. He combined that career with his family's farming history — and his own experience as a rancher in Mendocino County, California — to start Ranch Systems in 2005. He lives in Novato, California.

JC

Jacob Christfort

Founder & CEO

  • B.S. & M.S., Computer Science — University of Copenhagen
  • M.B.A. — Harvard Business School
  • 12 issued patents
  • Former CTO, Mobile & Wireless — Oracle Corporation

Why growers partner with us

A platform built to scale with you

Modular by design

Start with one sensor on one tank, and expand to a farm-wide network without replacing the platform underneath it.

Global support network

Authorized resellers and regional offices across North America, Latin America, Europe, and Australia keep installations running locally.

OEM & private-label programs

Manufacturers looking to add telemetry and sensor technology to their own products can license the platform under private label.

Want to partner with us?

Home  /  About Us  /  FAQ

FAQ

Questions, answered

The practical stuff people usually ask before requesting a demo — connectivity, installation, sensors, and support.

Request a Demo Jump to Questions

Yes. RS130, RS330, and RM400 all connect over cellular, so most sites never need WiFi at all. RM400 also supports WiFi if you'd rather use an existing connection at a shed or pump house.

Usually not for the telemetry hardware itself — RS130 and RS330 are solar and battery powered, with no electrical wiring required. If you're using RM400 to control valves, pumps, or other equipment, that wiring may need a licensed electrician depending on your existing setup and local code. Your authorized reseller can tell you exactly what your site needs before installation.

Each unit keeps logging locally, so you don't lose readings during a dropout — once signal returns, the backlog syncs to myRanch automatically. myRanch also flags offline devices, so you and your reseller know right away if something needs attention.

Yes — that's the whole idea behind the platform. RM400 and RS330 both have expansion slots for additional relays or sensors, and you can add entirely new units to the same myRanch account as your operation grows, from one tank to a farm-wide network.

Both go through the same myRanch app. RM400, RS130, and RS330 are real-time telemetry — readings show up live the moment a device connects. RanchDL is the RS10 running in data-logger mode instead of real-time transmission — a lower-cost option for sites that don't need continuous live monitoring, logging locally for download on your own schedule.

RS330 units can link together into a RanchMesh™ network, relaying data from node to node until it reaches one with a usable signal — built specifically for large or cellular-poor properties where a single unit wouldn't have reliable coverage on its own.

Ranch Systems hardware supports a wide range of third-party probes, including Aquacheck, METER Group, Sentek Drill & Drop, John Deere Field Connect, FloraPulse, and HSTI HydraSCOUT — connected directly or wirelessly via the RS10 sensor transmitter.

Only people you grant access to. myRanch is a secure, login-protected dashboard — you control who on your team or at your reseller can view or control your devices.

You don't have to do it alone. Our network of authorized resellers handles installation, configuration, and ongoing support in most regions — find one near you on our Find a Reseller page. In California, you also have the option of working directly with Ranch Systems for installation, support, and maintenance.

Yes. Ranch Systems has regional offices and authorized resellers across North America, Latin America, Europe, and Australia — find yours on our Find a Reseller page. Wherever you are, you're always welcome to reach us directly too — we may point you to your regional reseller, but our support line and email are open to anyone.

Still have a question?

Request a Demo Contact Support
